findings: Design: new cmdStatusAll() in scripts/keel-state.cjs placed after cmdStatus; dispatcher intercept `if (cmd === 'status' && storyId === '--all') { cmdStatusAll(); process.exit(0); }` inserted between the `!storyId` guard (line 652) and the switch (line 653), mirroring the existing memory-check early-exit idiom — runs before readManifest can die on a literal '--all' lookup.

Enumeration: non-recursive readdir of .keel/state/ with withFileTypes; stories = directories containing manifest.json only; files, .lock, *.tmp, and a literal '--all' dir skipped (B-4/B-6/BR-6); snapshots/ never surfaces since it lives inside story dirs.

Corrupt manifests use a local try/catch (skip-and-mark, B-5) — deliberately NOT readJson()/readManifest(), which die(1) and would abort the sweep; the sole die is B-9 (state root unreadable), giving BR-1's unconditional exit-0 otherwise.

Lock-free read-only is safe: writeManifest() is tmp+renameSync (atomic on NTFS and POSIX, keel-state.cjs:70-78), so a reader never sees a torn manifest; taking N locks would let a busy/wedged story block or fail the sweep, contradicting BR-1. Recorded as ADR-001.

Field projection exactly {story_id, scope (default 'feature'), current_phase (?? null), halted (=== true)} per BR-2/AC-1; no sequencing-gap evaluation in --all mode (B-8) so exit-0 stays simple; output sorted via localeCompare (BR-5), JSON.stringify(...,null,2) matching single-story format.

AC-3 guard is structural: cmdStatus, its switch case, and the !storyId dispatcher check are byte-for-byte untouched; the new path is only reachable via the new branch. USAGE string updated to 'status <story-id>|--all' — lesson L-1 checked, no hard-coded paths added to user-facing strings.

Impact analysis: .keel/graph/codegraph.json absent (Node engine repo); manual scan shows status consumers are commands/health.md step 1 (the requesting feature) and human CLI use; cmdStatus has no internal callers beyond the dispatcher. Blast radius: one file, additive. DB schema and API contract: n/a (local CLI, stdout/exit-code contract per B-1..B-10).

Test plan maps suite assertions to behaviors: 2-story fixture proves B-1/BR-2/BR-5; absent and empty state dir prove B-2/B-3; corrupt-manifest-plus-healthy-sibling proves B-5; root artifact filtering proves B-4/B-6; halted/defect fixtures prove B-7/B-8; unchanged single-story deep object and exit-1 on missing manifest plus full green engine suite prove B-10/AC-3.
decisions: ADR-001: status --all is lock-free read-only with skip-and-mark corrupt-manifest handling; only nonzero exit is an unreadable .keel/state/ root
Dispatcher intercept placed as an early-exit line before the switch, after the !storyId guard ('--all' is truthy so the guard needs no change)
cmdStatusAll uses local try/catch per story instead of readJson/readManifest to preserve B-5; comment in code prevents refactor regression
No new dependencies, no new files in the engine; single additive change to scripts/keel-state.cjs plus test suite additions
Future fleet commands (e.g. verify --all) must follow the ADR-001 pattern
